rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE CHU VS. JP MORGAN CHASE NATIONAL CORPORATE SERVICES, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint For Damages And Equitable Relief; Or Alternatively Motion For Dismissal For Failure To Timely File Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 06, 2014 line 1. DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'s Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint For Damages And Equitable Relief; Or Alternatively Motion For Dismissal For Failure To Timely File Amended Complaint CONTINUED to November 20, 2014. Moving party failed to provide the Court with courtesy copies. (See SF Local Rule 2.6B.) Courtesy copies must be dropped off in Dept. 501 by 4 p.m. on November 14, 2014 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date.= (501/LOT)- LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: DIONE POE et al VS. LAWRENCE CHOY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Application For Good Faith Settlement Determination
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 06, 2014 line 2. DEFENDANT CHRISTINA SUTTON's Application For Good Faith Settlement Determination Defendant Sutton's Unopposed Application for Good Faith Settlement Determination is GRANTED. = (501/LOT)- LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: DENISE BATTISTA VS. NORTHPOINT LAND LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Mental Examination
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 06, 2014 line 3. DEFENDANTS NP APARTMENTS, LLC and WILSEY MANAGEMENT, INC.'s Motion For Mental Examination GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The Court orders Plaintiff's mental examination by Dr. Mark Strassberg, as the expert of Defendants' choosing, without any temporal limitations and without the presence of counsel or court reporter. Defendants shall provide Plaintiff with an audio recording of the examination and any and all written reports and scoring results of the standardized tests. Defendant's examination of Plaintiff's sexual history shall be limited to discovery directly relevant to Plaintiff's claim. = (501/LOT)- LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: DIANE SCHOBER et al VS. KAMRAN ARDEBILCHI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To Amend Complaint; Ntc Of Application And Application For An Order Shortening Deft'S Time To Respond To Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 06, 2014 line 4. PLAINTIFFS JOAN ESPOSITO, BARBARA BRADSHAW, URAIWAN TORSAP, FAHD BINHAMAD, KHALID ALHABESH and UTHUMPORN DRAPER's Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint; Notice Of Application And Application For An Order Shortening Defendnat's Time To Respond To Second Amended Complaint OFF CALENDAR since the issue is now moot. = (501/LOT)- LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: DIANE SCHOBER et al VS. KAMRAN ARDEBILCHI et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 06, 2014 line 4. DEFENDANTS KAMRAN ARDEBILCHI and JAHANGIR ARDEBILCHI's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT GRANTED. Ps failed to allege sufficient factual assertions demonstrating that Ds' conduct was malicious, fraudulent, or oppressive. = (501/LOT)- LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: DIANE SCHOBER et al VS. KAMRAN ARDEBILCHI et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 06, 2014 line 4. DEFENDANTS KAMRAN ARDEBILCHI and JAHANGIR ARDEBILCHI's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Ps failed to plead with reasonable particularity the facts supporting the allegation. Additionally, Ps failed to specify when the action accrued so as to properly identify the SOL and Ps may not have standing pursuant to § 17200 as they did not sufficiently allege injury in fact. = (501/LOT)- LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: LARRY DUNN VS. MONTGOMERY REALTY GROUP, INC., A NEVADA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order To Expunge Notice Of Pending Action
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 6, 2014, Line 6: DEFENDANT MONTGOMERY REALTY GROUP's Motion For Order To Expunge Notice Of Pending Action: DENIED. rulings.Case Title: VICTORIANO BUSTO et al VS. ALEJANDRO V. DIESTA JR. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Further Responses To His Demand For Production, Inspection And Copying Of Documents-Set As From Pltffs, And Each Of Them; Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 6, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANT ALEX DIESTA JUNIOR's Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To His Demand For Production, Inspection And Copying Of Documents and For Monetary Sanctions: GRANTED in part and DENIED in part insofar as they request information about Plaintiff's income and assets, financial arrangements between Plaintiff's regarding joint ownership, other tenants' evictions and the construction project to renovate the building. Sanctions are awarded in the sum of $1,505.00. (501/LOT) LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: MARTHA MIRANDA VS. JESSICA KRIEGER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Martha Miranda And Rosa Marcano And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 6, 2014, Line 8: DEFENDANT GESSIKA KRIEGER's Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Martha Miranda And Rosa Marcano And For Sanctions GRANTED pursuant to CCP 2025.450(a). The depositions shall be taken by November 12, 2014 unless a different date is agreed to by the parties. There is no requirement for counsel to produce Martha Miranda for deposition in California. However, if this case proceeds to trial and Martha Miranda intends to testify, Ms. Miranda must make herself available in California for deposition prior to the commencement of trial. Sanctions are ordered in the sum of $1,950.00 as requested by Defendant. (501/LOT) LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 6, 2014, Line 10: DEFENDANTS RAUL HERNANDEZ and OSCAR WONG's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND since P failed to comply with requirements of CCP § 1166(a) and attach a copy of the notice of termination served on the Defendant upon which the Complaint is based.
